2605.06642.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
2605.06642.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as interactive agents, but optimizing them for long-horizon decision making remains difficult because current methods are largely purely reactive, which weakens both exploration and credit assignment over extended trajectories. In this work, we present Strategic Trajectory Abstraction (StraTA), a simple framework that introduces an explicit trajectory-level strategy into agentic reinforcement learning (RL). StraTA samples a compact strategy from the initial task state, conditions subsequent actions on that strategy, and trains strategy generation and action execution jointly with a hierarchical GRPO-style rollout design, further enhanced by diverse strategy rollout and critical self-judgment. Experiments on ALFWorld, WebShop, and SciWorld show that StraTA consistently improves both sample efficiency and final performance over strong baselines. StraTA reaches success rates of 93.1% on ALFWorld and 84.2% on WebShop. On SciWorld, StraTA attains a 63.5% overall score, outperforming frontier closed-source models.
2605.06642.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用作互動代理，但為長期決策優化它們仍然困難，因為當前的方法大多是純反應性的，這削弱了在延長軌跡上的探索和信用分配。在這項工作中，我們提出了戰略軌跡抽象（StraTA），這是一個簡單的框架，將明確的軌跡級策略引入代理強化學習（RL）。StraTA 從初始任務狀態中抽樣出一個緊湊的策略，並根據該策略條件後續行動，並與層次化的 GRPO 風格的展開設計共同訓練策略生成和行動執行，進一步通過多樣的策略展開和關鍵自我評判來增強。對 ALFWorld、WebShop 和 SciWorld 的實驗表明，StraTA 始終在強基準上提高了樣本效率和最終性能。StraTA 在 ALFWorld 上達到了 93.1% 的成功率，在 WebShop 上達到了 84.2%。在 SciWorld 上，StraTA 獲得了 63.5% 的總分，超越了前沿的封閉源模型。
2605.06642.author: Xiangyuan Xue et.al.
2605.06642.authors: Xiangyuan Xue, Yifan Zhou, Zidong Wang, Shengji Tang, Philip Torr, Wanli Ouyang, Lei Bai, Zhenfei Yin
